DISCOVERY, 612, PRESENT: BRYAN K. ANDERSON, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, DEL MONTE CORPORATION. LINDA H. WYNER, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. MOTION ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AUGMENT SUPPLEMENTAL EXPERT WITNESS LIST PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 2034(K) IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO DESIGNATE A SENSORY EVALUATION EXPERT WAS NOT THE RESULT OF MISTAKE, INADVERTANCE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD NOT IN THE EXERCISE OF REASONABLE DILIGENCE HAVE DETERMINED TO OFFER DIFFERENT OR ADDITIONAL EXPERT TESTIMONY. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT THE BIANCHI TESTIMONY WAS A SURPRISE OR THAT IT INTRODUCED NEW EVIDENCE NOT REASONABLY DISCLOSED THROUGH OTHER DISCOVERY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A.
